entityName: Anohana: The Flower We Saw That Day

metadata.entityName: Anohana: The Flower We Saw That Day

ingredients.name: Costa Resort Hanno
ingredients.address: 東町-8-8-4 Azumacho, Hanno, Saitama 357-0034, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This resort in Hanno appears in episode 5 as the destination where the group travels together, part of the outing that brings the Super Peace Busters closer while searching for a way to grant Menma's wish.

ingredients.name: Snack MIYUKI
ingredients.entityContext: This Chichibu snack bar appears as a background street scene in episode 2, part of the real town fabric that gives Anohana its grounded, small-city atmosphere.

ingredients.name: Ryusei Yagura
ingredients.entityContext: The Ryusei launching scaffold at Muku Shrine (Yoshidamuku Shrine) is the model for the fireworks scene that drives the climax of the series. The real annual Ryusei Festival, where bamboo rockets are fired skyward, directly inspired the Super Peace Busters' plan to launch a firework in Menma's honor.

ingredients.name: Yomota Glass Shop
ingredients.entityContext: This glass and building-materials shop on a Chichibu side street appears in episode 1 as part of the real-town streetscape Jintan walks through, grounding the anime's fictional Chichibu in the actual city.

ingredients.name: Koinrandoridepochichibuhinodaten
ingredients.localName: コインランドリーデポ秩父日野田店
ingredients.entityContext: This coin laundry appears as a background street landmark in episode 2, one of many everyday Chichibu businesses that the anime faithfully reproduced to create its authentic small-town setting.

ingredients.name: Oshima Landscape Supply
ingredients.entityContext: This landscaping supply yard near central Chichibu is visible as a background location in episode 1, part of the everyday residential streets Jintan wanders through in the series' opening moments.

ingredients.name: Near the Patisserie Lurian
ingredients.localName: パティスリー ルリアン付近
ingredients.entityContext: The street in front of this Chichibu patisserie is used as a background location in episode 7, reflecting the anime's practice of faithfully recreating the town's Sakuragimachi shopping area.

ingredients.name: Near the Araiya Sake Shop
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-13-1 Itabashi, Itabashi City, Tokyo 173-0004, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The street near this sake shop in northern Chichibu appears in episode 11 as the characters move through the town in the emotionally charged final arc, building toward the series climax.

ingredients.name: Seibu-Chichibu Station
ingredients.entityContext: Seibu-Chichibu Station is the gateway to the entire Anohana pilgrimage route and appears in the series as the main transit hub the group uses. The station's tourist information office distributes free Anohana pilgrimage maps, making it the natural starting point for any fan visit to Chichibu.

ingredients.name: Monmart Inoue
ingredients.type: コンビニエンスストア
categories: コンビニエンスストア
ingredients.entityContext: This local convenience store in Chichibu appears in episode 5 as a background location, one of the everyday shops that populate the show's loving recreation of real Chichibu town streets.

ingredients.name: Chichibu Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: The modern Chichibu Bridge over the Arakawa River appears in episode 10 as the group moves through the heightened emotional tension of the final arc. It pairs with the nearby Old Chichibu Bridge as a recurring riverside landmark throughout the series.

ingredients.name: Unicus Chichibu
ingredients.type: ショッピング モール
categories: ショッピング モール
ingredients.entityContext: Unicus Chichibu, the town's main shopping center, appears in episode 3 as a background location the characters pass through, reflecting the series' detailed recreation of everyday Chichibu life.

ingredients.name: Sakuragi Salon
ingredients.localName: ＣＰコスメティクス取扱店ＣＰウィズ桜木サロン
ingredients.type: エステティック サロン
categories: エステティック サロン
ingredients.entityContext: This beauty salon on Sakuragimachi street appears in episode 9 as part of the Chichibu townscape, located near the Jorin-ji and Keyaki Park cluster that anchors the show's Sakuragimachi scenes.

ingredients.name: Chichibu Shrine
ingredients.entityContext: Chichibu Shrine, a major Shinto site at the heart of the city, appears in episode 4 and provides the spiritual backdrop that mirrors the supernatural thread of Menma's lingering presence running through the story.

ingredients.name: Hot Spot Chichibu-kan
ingredients.localName: ほっとすぽっと秩父館
ingredients.entityContext: This tourist building on Miyakawacho appears in episode 2 as a Chichibu streetscape landmark. Its Miyakawacho location, just north of Chichibu Shrine, places it in the middle of the central pilgrimage walking route.

ingredients.name: Drugstore Seki Chichibu Shop
ingredients.localName: ドラッグストアセキ 秩父店
ingredients.entityContext: This drugstore on Miyakawacho is visible in episode 1 as part of the Chichibu streetscape, one of the recognizable storefronts the animators reproduced to establish the show's real-town setting from the very first episode.

ingredients.name: Old Chichibu Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: The Old Chichibu Bridge (Kyu Chichibu-bashi) is the single most iconic Anohana location, featured prominently in the opening theme "Aoi Shiori" and appearing as a recurring meeting place throughout the series. The arched concrete span over the Arakawa River is the heart of every fan's pilgrimage route and considered unmissable for anyone visiting Chichibu.

ingredients.name: Hitsujiyama Park
ingredients.entityContext: Hitsujiyama Park's Shibazakura Hill, covered in 400,000 moss phlox blooms each spring, is the backdrop for the opening sequence where Menma stands in a field of flowers. The image of the pink hillside became one of the most recognized visuals of the series and draws devoted fans every April during the Shibazakura Festival.

ingredients.name: Hachimanya Honten
ingredients.entityContext: This traditional wagashi confectionery shop near Chichibu Shrine appears in episode 4, its old-fashioned facade contributing to the nostalgic, small-town Chichibu atmosphere that defines the series' visual identity.

ingredients.name: Chichibu Festival Museum
ingredients.entityContext: The Chichibu Festival Museum, dedicated to the city's famous Yomatsuri float festival, appears in episode 1 as a location Jintan passes through. The real Chichibu Night Festival tradition also informs the fireworks and celebration themes woven throughout the anime.

ingredients.name: Yoshidamuku Shrine
ingredients.entityContext: Muku Shrine (Yoshidamuku Shrine) is the setting for the climactic fireworks scene in episode 11, where the Super Peace Busters launch a rocket in Menma's honor. The shrine's real annual Ryusei Festival, featuring bamboo rockets fired from a wooden scaffold, directly inspired this defining moment of the series.

ingredients.name: Printemps
ingredients.type: デザート ショップ
categories: デザート ショップ
ingredients.entityContext: This dessert shop on Sakuragimachi appears in episode 3, part of the Chichibu streetscape scenes. Its coordinates place it in the Sakuragimachi cluster near Jorin-ji, within the core of the Anohana pilgrimage walking route.

ingredients.name: Small Dream Toy Box Tatsumiya
ingredients.localName: 小さな夢のおもちゃ箱 たつみや
ingredients.entityContext: This charming toy shop on Miyakawacho appears in episode 2 as a background street location, part of the old-fashioned commercial district the animators used to anchor Anohana's sense of a nostalgic, real-feeling small town.

ingredients.name: Hanno Station
ingredients.entityContext: Hanno Station appears in episode 5 as the transit point for the group's excursion away from Chichibu, part of the outing that takes several characters to the nearby resort area as they struggle to understand Menma's wishes.

ingredients.name: Jorin-ji
ingredients.address: コガク三田, 4 Chome-5-14 Mita, Minato City, Tokyo 108-0073, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Jorin-ji is the spiritual heart of the Anohana pilgrimage, featured in the opening and throughout the series as the temple where the Super Peace Busters played hide-and-seek as children. The stone steps and grounds appear multiple times as the group reconnects, and visiting fans leave Anohana-themed ema wishing plaques at the 17th stop on the Chichibu 34 Kannon pilgrimage.

ingredients.name: Pink Moss Hill
ingredients.entityContext: The Shibazakura (Pink Moss) Hill within Hitsujiyama Park is the specific location depicted when Menma stands in a vivid field of pink flowers in episode 2. This hillside, carpeted with 400,000 moss phlox blooms each spring, is one of the most-photographed Anohana pilgrimage spots.

ingredients.name: Chichibu Daiichi Hospital
ingredients.entityContext: Chichibu Daiichi Hospital appears in episode 10 as the tense emotional arc nears its resolution, part of the real Chichibu institutional landscape the animators incorporated to keep the setting grounded.

ingredients.name: Beniya Sakuragi Store
ingredients.entityContext: This clothing store on Sakuragimachi appears in episode 1 as a background streetscape location, part of the Chichibu commercial district the animation team documented in detail to authenticate the show's setting.

ingredients.name: Kinhisashi Clock Shop
ingredients.entityContext: This old-fashioned clock and eyeglass shop on Nakamachi appears in episode 3 as a background street scene, one of the characterful local businesses that give Chichibu's commercial district its distinctive retro personality in the anime.

ingredients.name: Miyakawacho Jido Park
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-4-10 Omiya, Suginami City, Tokyo 168-0061, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This small neighborhood park on Miyakawacho is depicted in episode 8 as a quiet outdoor space in the Chichibu town center, reflecting the series' attention to the mundane pockets of the city where its characters drift.

ingredients.name: Near the Chichibu City No.1 Kyudojo
ingredients.localName: 秩父市第一弓道場付近
ingredients.entityContext: The area near Chichibu's municipal archery range appears in episode 3 as a background outdoor location, one of the northern Chichibu spots the anime reproduced as the characters move through different parts of the city.

ingredients.name: Yao Department
ingredients.entityContext: Yao Department Store, the main department store in Chichibu, appears in episode 3 and serves as a recognizable landmark of the city's shopping district. Its prominent building on Kamimachi helped the animators establish geographic continuity in the town's layout.

ingredients.name: Belc Higashimachi Shop
ingredients.entityContext: This Belc supermarket on Higashimachi appears in episode 7 as a recognizable everyday location in the Chichibu townscape, part of the show's commitment to faithfully representing the city's southern commercial streets.

ingredients.name: Keyaki Park
ingredients.entityContext: Keyaki Park sits directly behind Jorin-ji temple and appears in episode 9 as the park bench where Jintan sits, a quiet spot that connects to the Jorin-ji grounds where the Super Peace Busters spent their childhood. Fans often visit both spots together on the pilgrimage walk.

ingredients.name: Chichibu Aioicho Postal Housing
ingredients.localName: 秩父市相生町郵政宿舎
ingredients.entityContext: This postal housing block in Aioicho appears in episode 7 as a residential background location, part of the quiet Chichibu neighborhood streets that give the series its sense of an ordinary town carrying extraordinary grief.

ingredients.name: asaka chouchin ten
ingredients.entityContext: This traditional paper lantern craft shop on Miyakawacho appears in episode 1, its handmade goods a fitting visual note in an anime set against the backdrop of Chichibu's festival culture and the lantern-lit memory of Menma.
